front: Create a namespace called `mynamespace`.
back:
```bash
kubectl create namespace mynamespace
```

**Description:** Creates a new namespace, a virtual cluster used to scope and isolate groups of resources.

[Namespaces](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/namespaces/)

front: Get the YAML for a new namespace called `myns` **without** creating it.
back:
```bash
kubectl create namespace myns --dry-run=client -o yaml
```

**Description:** `--dry-run=client` renders the manifest locally without contacting the API server, and `-o yaml` prints it. A fast way to bootstrap a manifest.

[kubectl Cheat Sheet](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubectl/cheatsheet/)

front: List all pods across **all** namespaces.
back:
```bash
kubectl get po -A
```

**Description:** `-A` (alias for `--all-namespaces`) lists resources cluster-wide instead of only the current namespace.

[kubectl Cheat Sheet](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubectl/cheatsheet/)

front: Create a pod named `nginx` using image `nginx`.
back:
```bash
kubectl run nginx --image=nginx --restart=Never
```

**Description:** `kubectl run` with `--restart=Never` creates a bare Pod (not a Deployment or Job).

[kubectl run](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubectl/kubectl-commands#run)

front: Create a pod named `nginx` (image `nginx`) in the `mynamespace` namespace.
back:
```bash
kubectl run nginx --image=nginx --restart=Never -n mynamespace
```

**Description:** `-n` / `--namespace` targets a specific namespace for the created resource.

[Namespaces](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/namespaces/)

front: Generate the YAML for an `nginx` pod **without** creating it.
back:
```bash
kubectl run nginx --image=nginx --restart=Never --dry-run=client -o yaml
```

**Description:** Combine `kubectl run` with `--dry-run=client -o yaml` to scaffold a Pod manifest you can save and edit (`> pod.yaml`).

[kubectl Cheat Sheet](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubectl/cheatsheet/)

front: Create a pod named `nginx` (image `nginx`) and expose container traffic on port 80.
back:
```bash
kubectl run nginx --image=nginx --restart=Never --port=80
```

**Description:** `--port` declares the container port in the Pod spec (does not create a Service).

[kubectl run](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubectl/kubectl-commands#run)

front: Create an `nginx` pod and set an environment variable `var1=val1`.
back:
```bash
kubectl run nginx --image=nginx --restart=Never --env=var1=val1
```

**Description:** `--env` injects an environment variable into the container; repeat the flag for more variables.

[Define Environment Variables for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/)

front: Create a `busybox` pod that runs the command `env` (and see its output).
back:
```bash
kubectl run busybox --image=busybox --command --restart=Never -it --rm -- env
```

**Description:** Everything after `--` is the container command; `--command` overrides the image entrypoint, `-it` streams output, and `--rm` deletes the pod on exit.

[kubectl run](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubectl/kubectl-commands#run)

front: Run a `busybox` pod that echoes `hello world`, then have it deleted automatically when it completes.
back:
```bash
kubectl run busybox --image=busybox -it --rm --restart=Never -- echo 'hello world'
```

**Description:** `--rm` removes the pod once it terminates, ideal for throwaway one-off tasks.

[kubectl run](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubectl/kubectl-commands#run)

front: Get a pod's full YAML definition (pod `nginx`).
back:
```bash
kubectl get po nginx -o yaml
```

**Description:** `-o yaml` outputs the live object, including status and defaulted fields.

[kubectl Cheat Sheet](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubectl/cheatsheet/)

front: Print only the image of pod `nginx` using JSONPath.
back:
```bash
kubectl get po nginx -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[].image}'
```

**Description:** `-o jsonpath` extracts a single field from the object, useful for scripting and quick checks.

[JSONPath Support](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubectl/jsonpath/)

front: Get detailed information and events for pod `nginx` (e.g. why it hasn't started).
back:
```bash
kubectl describe po nginx
```

**Description:** `describe` shows a human-readable summary plus the Events section, the first stop when debugging a stuck pod.

[Application Introspection and Debugging](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/debug/debug-application/debug-running-pod/)

front: Get pod `nginx`'s IP address.
back:
```bash
kubectl get po nginx -o wide
```

**Description:** `-o wide` adds extra columns including the pod IP and the node it runs on.

[kubectl Cheat Sheet](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubectl/cheatsheet/)

front: Get the logs of pod `nginx`.
back:
```bash
kubectl logs nginx
```

**Description:** Streams stdout/stderr of the (single) container in the pod.

[kubectl logs](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubectl/kubectl-commands#logs)

front: A pod crashed and restarted — get the logs of its **previous** instance (pod `nginx`).
back:
```bash
kubectl logs nginx --previous
```

**Description:** `--previous` (`-p`) returns logs from the prior terminated container, essential for diagnosing crash loops.

[kubectl logs](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubectl/kubectl-commands#logs)

front: Open an interactive shell inside pod `nginx`.
back:
```bash
kubectl exec -it nginx -- /bin/sh
```

**Description:** `exec -it` attaches an interactive TTY; everything after `--` is run inside the container.

[Get a Shell to a Running Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/debug/debug-application/get-shell-running-container/)

front: Show the environment variables inside running pod `nginx`.
back:
```bash
kubectl exec -it nginx -- env
```

**Description:** Runs `env` once inside the container to verify injected variables without opening a shell.

[Get a Shell to a Running Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/debug/debug-application/get-shell-running-container/)

front: Change pod `nginx`'s image to `nginx:1.24.0`.
back:
```bash
kubectl set image pod/nginx nginx=nginx:1.24.0
```

**Description:** Syntax is `pod/POD_NAME CONTAINER_NAME=IMAGE:TAG`; the container is killed and recreated with the new image.

[kubectl set image](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubectl/kubectl-commands#set)

front: Forward local port 8080 to port 80 of pod `nginx`.
back:
```bash
kubectl port-forward pod/nginx 8080:80
```

**Description:** Tunnels a local port to a port on the pod, letting you reach the app at `localhost:8080` without a Service.

[Use Port Forwarding to Access Applications in a Cluster](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/access-application-cluster/port-forward-access-application-cluster/)

front: Get the YAML for a ResourceQuota `myrq` with hard limits of 1 CPU, 1G memory and 2 pods **without** creating it.
back:
```bash
kubectl create quota myrq --hard=cpu=1,memory=1G,pods=2 --dry-run=client -o yaml
```

**Description:** A ResourceQuota caps aggregate resource consumption in a namespace; `--hard` lists the constraints as comma-separated key=value pairs.

[Resource Quotas](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/policy/resource-quotas/)
